name: firebase-development-validate
description: This skill should be used when reviewing Firebase code against security model and best practices. Triggers on "review firebase", "check firebase", "validate", "audit firebase", "security review", "look at firebase code". Validates configuration, rules, architecture, and security.
capabilities: Validates firebase.json configuration structure and hosting patterns
Reviews Firestore security rules for common vulnerabilities
Checks functions architecture consistency across Express, domain-grouped, or individual patterns
Verifies authentication implementation for API keys and Firebase Auth
Confirms ABOUTME documentation headers and test coverage requirements
Audits production readiness including build, tests, and secrets management
author: 2389-research
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based documentation skill with no executable code. The static scanner flagged Markdown backtick syntax (for file paths, skill references, and example commands) as shell execution patterns - these are false positives. No network access, file system modifications, or actual command execution capabilities exist. The skill only provides validation guidance for Claude to follow when reviewing Firebase projects.
valueStatement: Firebase projects often have misconfigurations that lead to security vulnerabilities. This skill provides a systematic validation checklist covering Firestore rules, functions architecture, authentication patterns, and production readiness checks.
userTitle: Validate Firebase code security and architecture
limitations: Does not modify code or fix issues automatically
Cannot access external Firebase projects without explicit file sharing
Does not execute tests or deploy to Firebase
Validates based on documented patterns, not runtime behavior

useCases.title: Security audit preparation
useCases.description: Review Firebase projects before production deployment for security gaps and misconfigurations

useCases.title: Firebase code review
useCases.description: Systematically validate Firebase code against established architecture and security patterns

useCases.title: Production readiness check
useCases.description: Verify Firebase projects meet deployment standards for build, tests, and configuration

promptTemplates.title: Basic validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate this Firebase project for security issues and best practices compliance

promptTemplates.title: Rules review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review firestore.rules for vulnerabilities and check that default deny is present

promptTemplates.title: Architecture check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if our Cloud Functions follow a consistent architecture pattern

promptTemplates.title: Full audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a complete Firebase security audit including hosting, functions, firestore, authentication, and production readiness

bestPractices: Run validation before every production deployment to catch security issues early
Fix critical security findings immediately before proceeding with development
Use the provided checklists to ensure consistent review coverage across all Firebase services
Combine with emulator testing to validate behavior alongside static pattern analysis
antiPatterns: Skipping validation when deadlines are tight - security issues cost more to fix later
Ignoring missing ABOUTME comments as minor issues - they indicate incomplete documentation
Deploying without verifying emulator configuration leads to unexpected production behavior
Assuming client-write rules are secure without diff().affectedKeys() validation creates vulnerabilities

faq.question: Does this skill modify my Firebase code?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads and analyzes your code. It provides recommendations but never makes changes to your files.

faq.question: What Firebase services does it validate?
faq.answer: Hosting configuration, Cloud Functions architecture, Firestore security rules, Authentication patterns, Emulator setup, and project security.

faq.question: Can it validate existing production projects?
faq.answer: Yes, share your firebase.json, firestore.rules, and functions source code for comprehensive analysis.

faq.question: Is my code or data sent anywhere?
faq.answer: No. All analysis happens locally within Claude Code. Nothing is transmitted to external servers.

faq.question: How long does validation take?
faq.answer: A complete audit takes 2-5 minutes depending on project size. Individual checks complete in under a minute.

faq.question: How does this compare to Firebase security rules analyzer?
faq.answer: This skill provides broader architecture review beyond just rules, including code patterns, project structure, and consistency checks.
